SCORE: 78/100
DATE SCORED: 2025-06-10
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 445

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (12/20) — Reviews mention a small playground and occasional live music/food truck events at Easter, but no water park, jumping pillow, or dedicated kids' entertainment facilities. Families report children enjoyed beach activities, fishing, crabbing, dolphins, pelicans, and seals more than structured entertainment. Some reviews note "limited options for children" and "not a lot for the kids" compared to modern resort parks.

Nature (19/20) — Outstanding natural environment with direct beachfront access, spectacular ocean views, and abundant wildlife including dolphins, pelicans, and resident seals at the jetty. Reviews consistently praise "right on the beach," "waterfront sites," "fishing off the beach," "beautiful coastal walks," and "amazing birdlife." Easy beach access for swimming, crabbing, and pontoon use. 

Cleanliness (15/15) — Exceptional cleanliness across all reviews. Consistent phrases include "spotlessly clean," "immaculate amenities," "always clean," "best kept park in 51 years of camping," and "facilities are brilliant & superbly maintained." Amenities, camp kitchen, cabins, and grounds all described as pristine and well-maintained.

Value (13/15) — Generally positive value comments with "well priced," "very affordable," and "reasonable rates" mentioned frequently. Some reviews note it's comparable in price to parks with pools/splash zones despite lacking those facilities. Off-peak specials praised. One negative mentioned unpowered Oyster Point site "not value for money" at peak time.

Site Size (7/10) — Mixed feedback on site size. Beachfront sites praised for views but described as "tight," "very tight fit," "smaller sites," "narrow roads," and "difficult getting on site." One 1-star review specifically cited site too small for awning. Staff offer tractor assistance for positioning vans. Some grass sites described as spacious, particularly at Oyster Point.

Sentiment (10/10) — Overwhelmingly positive sentiment with massive repeat visitation. Reviews mention returning for 15-60 years, "always coming back," "our happy place," "home away from home," "favourite caravan park," and "wouldn't stay anywhere else." Managers Anita and Ash frequently praised by name for exceptional customer service.

Location (10/10) — Perfect beachfront location with easy walking distance (5 minutes) to town shops, pub, cafe, supermarket, and jetty. Central base for exploring southern Yorke Peninsula. Reviews praise "ideal location," "fantastic location," "perfect location," and "well situated to explore." Beach access directly from sites.

WHY NOT 100:
1. No water play facilities (pool, splash park, or waterslides) and limited structured kids' entertainment compared to modern family resort parks, with only a small playground and occasional special events rather than daily activities.
2. Site size constraints with tight access, narrow roads, and some sites too small for large rigs with awnings out, particularly when parks at capacity with boats and vehicles, requiring staff tractor assistance for positioning.
3. Split facility confusion with separate Oyster Point location causing booking allocation issues, incomplete maps, and inconsistent site quality (some unpowered sites on dirt vs grass), plus wind exposure on beachfront sites requiring proper camping preparation.

Multiple reviews mention kids riding around, playing, fishing on the jetty, crabbing at low tide, swimming in the netted ocean enclosure, exploring rock pools, and giant chess. Some families noted the playground is "tiny" and suggested a pool or outdoor theatre would enhance entertainment options.

Nature (19/20) — Outstanding natural environment with direct beachfront access, stunning bay views, spectacular sunsets over Spencer Gulf, a nearby jetty for fishing and squidding, safe netted ocean swimming area, and rock pools. Families love the beach walks, the wide sandy beach, and the coastal setting. The natural beauty and ocean access are consistently praised as major highlights.

Cleanliness (14/15) — The park receives high marks for cleanliness with reviews describing it as "the cleanest caravan park I've ever been to," "spotless," and "super clean." Amenities are cleaned multiple times daily and bathrooms are well-maintained. However, several reviews mentioned amenities "need a bit of love," are "a tad old," or "in need of an update," and one review cited leaking showers wasting water, preventing a perfect score.

Value (11/15) — Mixed feedback on value with several families noting it's "pricey," "relatively expensive," and pricing is "dynamic" during peak times. Some felt prices were "a bit rich" though specials helped. Positive mentions of "very well priced," "prices are good," and "average value for money" appear, but the consensus is that while the location and views justify the cost, it's on the higher end for a council-owned park.

Site Size (8/10) — Sites receive generally positive feedback as "large sites," "great size sites," "big sites," and "spacious." Waterfront sites are particularly praised. However, some reviews noted sites can be "tight," one guest said it felt like "a sardine can," and tent campers found the ground "rock solid." Caravan parking in cabin areas is described as "very inadequate" and "way too narrow for SUVs."

Sentiment (9/10) — Strong positive sentiment with many families returning annually (one for 21 years, another for 20 years, another 3 times). Guests frequently say they "can't wait to come back," "will definitely return," and "already booked for next year." However, multiple 1-star reviews detail poor customer service experiences with reception staff described as "extremely rude," "sour attitudes," "stand offish," and lacking compassion in difficult circumstances, which impacts overall sentiment.

Location (10/10) — Perfect score for location with the park right on the waterfront, walking distance to the jetty, splash park, cafe, beach, and netted swimming area. The beachfront position with stunning sunset views is repeatedly called "unreal," "spectacular," and "best location."

WHY NOT 100:
1. Customer service concerns are prominent with multiple recent reviews describing reception staff as rude, unhelpful, and lacking compassion, particularly during busy periods or when guests needed assistance with basic requests like clean towels or booking changes.
2. Value for money is a recurring issue with families noting the park is expensive, especially during peak times, and some felt the pricing didn't match the experience given it's council-owned with older amenities in some areas.

PARK: Port Broughton Tourist Park, Yorke Peninsula, South Australia
SCORE: 76/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-24
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 145

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (18/20) — Consistently praised for "water park, shaded swimming pool, jumping pillow, mini golf and play equipment", "skate park", pump track, and "so much for the kids to do". Families describe it as having "ALL the kids facilities and activities" with movies during school holidays. The waterpark receives particular acclaim as "fun for all ages" and a major drawcard.

Nature (16/20) — Beachfront Spencer Gulf location with "beautiful beach view", "stunning sunsets and sunrises", direct beach access for fishing and crabbing, and peaceful coastal atmosphere. Reviews mention "lovely walks along Spencer Gulf", native birdlife (wattle birds, honeyeaters, magpies), and dog-friendly beach areas. However, limited bushland and natural shade noted.

Cleanliness (10/15) — Highly polarized feedback creates significant concern. Many recent reviews praise "exceptionally clean and well maintained" facilities and "spotlessly clean" amenities. However, multiple detailed complaints describe "cabin was so dirty, floors hadn't been swept, mopped or vacuumed", "amenities block has not had a good clean for years, scum & mould build up", "toilets disgusting", and "camp kitchen putrid, floors look like someone tapdanced while cleaning 100 live squid". Staff response appears inconsistent.

Value (11/15) — Mixed perceptions with "definitely get your value for money" from repeat visitors, but pricing concerns emerge: "$60 per night powered sites" considered expensive by some, "not a bargain at $50 for a powered site", and "very expensive" for cabin quality received. Several guests felt premium pricing didn't match service/cleanliness standards delivered.

Site Size (9/10) — Strong performance with "caravan sites were huge", "sites are spacious plenty of room for the awning and car", "very large sites", and "good size sites" mentioned frequently. Both powered and beachfront sites described as generous, though some are synthetic grass/gravel rather than natural grass.

Sentiment (7/10) — Divided sentiment from families. Many 5-star "5th visit" regulars who "absolutely love going here for family holidays" and "will definitely be back". However, significant number of 1-star reviews stating "will not be going back", "wouldn't recommend at all", and complaints about poor customer service, particularly around cancellation policies and front desk interactions creating negative experiences.

Location (5/10) — "Easy walking distance to the Main Street and pub", "1km walk to town, pub and jetty", close to IGA supermarket and local amenities. However, Port Broughton itself is a small town with limited attractions beyond fishing/beach activities. Remote Yorke Peninsula location means considerable drive from Adelaide (approximately 2.5 hours) with limited nearby tourism infrastructure.

WHY NOT 100:
1. **Inconsistent cleanliness standards severely impact guest experience** — Multiple detailed reviews from 2024-2026 describe filthy cabins with dog fur, dirty sheets, mould in bathrooms, unswept floors, and poorly maintained amenities. One April 2026 review details "cabin was so dirty... dog fur everywhere, hair through sheets, walls hadn't had a scrub, dishes incredibly dirty" while a January 2025 review states "amenities block has not had a good clean for years, scum & mould build up in showers". This inconsistency creates an unacceptable risk for families.

2. **Dust and mosquito issues create environmental challenges** — Numerous reviews describe "dirt/gravel site that blows up so much dust, you'll be cleaning your gear for eternity", "dust bowl", "lots of dirt everywhere constantly", and "nothing can prepare you for the onslaught of mosquitoes at dusk". The coastal location attracts "tons of mozzies, flies and wind gust" making outdoor enjoyment difficult during peak season.

3. **Customer service and management concerns damage reputation** — Multiple reviews describe "very poor attitude towards people" from front desk staff, "unfriendly and unhelpful" service, guests being "evicted from the park" after complaints, "very rude manner" interactions, inflexible cancellation policies even for family deaths, and pricing discrepancies between online and counter rates. Management appears defensive rather than solution-focused.

Cleanliness (8/15) — Mixed feedback with some noting "spotlessly clean" facilities and deluxe cabins, but multiple recent reviews cite "dirty room with hair all over the pillows", "floor was duty", "quilt was duty", toilets with "no soap", "musty smell", and amenities "need proper ventilation" suggesting declining standards.

Value (7/15) — Several reviews mention "overpriced for room", complicated refund policies requiring council involvement, charging for bedding, unreasonable fees, and "cabin had the bare minimum". Some older reviews noted reasonable pricing but recent feedback suggests poor value given service issues.

Site Size (5/10) — Consistent complaints about "sites are small", "very small with nowhere to park", "designated places were too small to fit our cars", "choice of awning out and car on the street or car in and no awning", and "a bit squeezy if you have a larger van".
